title: 4 Uploading AFT Files
body_text:
4	Uploading AFT Files
4.1	Overview of Uploading AFT Files
Once the Originator creates an AFT file using in-house or third-party software, the Originator must upload the file to Central 1. For instructions on how to submit the AFT file to Central 1, refer to Section 4.3, AFT File Transmissions to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
4.2	Business Rules Pertaining to AFT File Transmissions
The Originator must adhere to the following business rules when uploading AFT files to Central 1 via file transmission:
Ensure that the appropriate agreements and application forms have been completed.
Obtain authorization for sporadic debits at every instance.
Submit the file in Central 1âs AFT File Format. This format is based on CPA Standard 005. See Section 2.4, AFT File Specifications.
Submit the file by 2 pm two business days before the due date. Files uploaded after the deadline may not be processed until the following business day.
Ensure that the file creation date is no more than seven days before the processing date.
Ensure that the files are submitted in consecutive file creation number order, starting at 0001. If more than one file is uploaded at a time, the files must be in sequential order.
Ensure that credit records are not post-dated beyond 45 days of the file creation date or backdated more than 30 calendar days. The CPA states that credit records cannot be post-dated beyond 14 days, but Central 1 will warehouse the transactions for up to 45 days.
Ensure that debit records are not post-dated beyond 45 days of the date the transaction is forwarded to the Processing Direct Clearer or backdated more than 173 calendar days. The CPA states that debit records cannot be post-dated beyond two days, but Central 1 will warehouse the transactions for up to 45 days.
Ensure that the total value of the file does not exceed the Originatorâs maximum file dollar amount.
4.3	AFT File Transmission to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange
To upload an AFT file to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange, the Originator must have access to the File and Report Exchange. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on obtaining access to the File and Report Exchange.
Once the Originator has access to the File and Report Exchange and has verified that Central 1 has set up an AFT subdirectory, the Originator proceeds as follows to upload and confirm AFT files:
Upload the AFT File
Connect to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange and open the AFT subdirectory.
Browse to find the file you want to send.
Upload the file to Central 1 according to the specifications of the FTP Client or alternate method of connection. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on uploading files to the File and Report Exchange.
Confirm that the AFT File Uploaded Successfully
Check that the following files are automatically created in the AFT directory a few minutes after uploading the file:
â
.LST
â Is always created and contains a message advising whether the file was successful
â
.ERR
â Is created only if an error exists in the file. It will advise you to refer to the .LST file for the error message. Correct the file and resend it to Central 1 via FTP
â
.BAK
â Is a back-up of the original AFT file
Always open the .LST file to confirm that the file was successfully uploaded.
Ensure that the file is successfully sent to Central 1 by 2 pm PT, two business days before the due date.
4.4	Processing Uploaded AFT Files
Central 1 does not notify the Originator of rejects when the file is initially uploaded to the File and Report Exchange. It is the Originatorâs responsibility to confirm that the file is uploaded successfully. Once a file is received on Central 1âs mainframe, it is validated. If the file rejects at this point, Central 1 will advise the Originator. The Originator then corrects the file and resends it.
Transactions within the file are subject to validation, first at Central 1 and then at the destination financial institution. Rejected transactions do not cause the whole AFT file to reject. They are reported on the Recalled/Rejected PAP Items Report (ICRR/UCRR). See the AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part.
See the Introduction to AFT Part for more information.
END
